entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: A type of soil characterized by high clay content and high water-holding capacity

entities.name: WATER-HOLDING CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to retain water, allowing crops to survive longer periods

entities.name: DRYLAND CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: Agricultural production in regions with limited or uncertain rainfall

entities.name: SEMI-ARID ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low and erratic rainfall

entities.name: HEAVY RAINFALL
entities.description: Intense precipitation events that can affect soil trafficability

entities.name: CLAY CONTENT
entities.description: The proportion of clay particles in the soil which influences physical properties

entities.name: MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of water present in the soil, affecting its workability

entities.name: TILLAGE AND PLANTING OPERATIONS
entities.description: Agricultural activities involving soil preparation and sowing

entities.name: DRAINAGE
entities.description: The ability of soil to allow water to flow out, which is often impeded in deep Vertisols

entities.name: AERATION
entities.description: The process of air circulation within the soil

entities.name: WATER INTAKE
entities.description: The rate at which soil absorbs water

entities.name: PERMEABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to allow liquids to pass through

entities.name: FERTILITY STATUS
entities.description: The chemical ability of soil to sustain plant growth

entities.name: LAND MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Strategies used to improve soil properties for better crop production
